reasoning: The resolution criteria require a US ally (NATO member or Major Non-NATO Ally) that currently does not possess nuclear weapons to acquire full, independent control of an operational nuclear weapon AND have this officially confirmed by December 31, 2025. Countries with pre-existing arsenals like Pakistan (and functionally Israel, France, UK) are excluded. Building a nuclear weapon from scratch takes years, making it practically impossible for highly capable allies like South Korea or Japan to complete the process—including NPT withdrawal, enrichment/reprocessing, weaponization, and official confirmation—within the remaining 9 months of 2025, even if there were a radical policy shift. Transferring full independent control of an existing US weapon is prohibited by US law and the NPT, and would face insurmountable political and institutional obstacles. Non-allies like Ukraine do not meet the strict MNNA/NATO definitional criteria. Therefore, the probability is negligible, effectively at the minimum bound of 0.02.
